title: Intensely Savory Mexican Pork Tacos Al Pastor
description: A savory and shattering Mexican dish featuring lemons and pork, perfect for a date night, served in individual mini cocottes and dairy-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together chili powder, cumin, coriander, and a pinch of salt.
Add the sliced pork to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork for 2-3 minutes per side, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced onions and cook until they are lightly charred, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the juiced lemons to the skillet and stir to combine with the onions and garlic.
Add the seared pork back to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and lemon mixture.
Transfer the pork mixture to individual mini cocottes and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the pork is tender and lightly browned.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by placing a portion of the pork mixture onto a warmed tortilla, and topping with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
